message.content:
<|channel>thought<channel|>### C# API Design: Resumable Benchmark Runner

To satisfy the requirement for a **resumable** runner within a **single-server** constraint (one heavy model at a time), the architecture must use a sequential task queue with a persistent state store.

#### 1. Core API Sketch

```csharp
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Threading;
using System.Threading.Tasks;

public record BenchmarkResult(double Score, DateTime CompletedAt);

public interface IBenchmarkRepository
{
    Task<IEnumerable<BenchmarkContext>> GetPendingAsync();
    Task SaveResultAsync(BenchmarkContext context, BenchmarkResult result);
    Task MarkAsRunningAsync(BenchmarkContext context);
}

public interface IModelServerHarness : IAsyncDisposable
{
    Task LoadModelAsync(string modelPath);
    Task<double[]> RunInferenceAsync(string prompt, IReadOnlyList<string> constraints);
}

public class BenchmarkRunner
{
    private readonly IBenchmarkRepository _repo;
    private readonly IModelServerHarness _harness;
    private readonly SemaphoreSlim _singleServerLock = new(1, 1);

    public BenchmarkRunner(IBenchmarkRepository repo, IModelServerHarness harness)
    {
        _repo = repo;
        _harness = harness;
    }

    public async Task RunAllAsync(CancellationToken ct)
    {
        // Ensure only one runner instance controls the heavy model server
        await _singleServerLock.WaitAsync(ct);
        try
        {
            var pending = await _repo.GetPendingAsync();
            foreach (var context in pending)
            {
                if (ct.IsCancellationRequested) break;

                await Execute
